edges.description: KIT signals through RAS to activate RAF-MEK-ERK cascade
KIT directly activates PI3K through phosphorylation of adapter proteins
KIT signaling also engages JAK-STAT transcriptional programs.
ERK activation drives cyclin D1 expression and cell cycle entry
AKT phosphorylates and inactivates pro-apoptotic factors
JAK-STAT signaling contributes to KIT-driven transcriptional programs.

nodes.description: PI3K-AKT activation downstream of KIT promotes melanoma cell survival through phosphorylation-mediated inactivation of pro-apoptotic proteins and activation of survival pathways.

evidence.reference_title: Imatinib targeting of KIT-mutant oncoprotein in melanoma.
evidence.snippet: In vitro analyses revealed major sensitivity to KIT kinase inhibition by imatinib, with potent induction of melanoma cell apoptosis.

nodes.description: KIT-mutant melanoma cells also show JAK-STAT pathway modulation downstream of KIT kinase activity, supporting transcriptional programs that complement MAPK and PI3K-AKT signaling.

evidence.reference_title: Imatinib targeting of KIT-mutant oncoprotein in melanoma.
evidence.snippet: Exposure to drug affected the mitogen-activated protein kinase, phosphatidylinositol 3-kinase/AKT, JAK-STAT, and antiapoptotic pathways.

nodes.description: KIT mutations, most commonly in exons 11, 13, and 17, result in constitutive receptor activation independent of stem cell factor (SCF) ligand binding. Mutations may cause ligand-independent dimerization, altered kinase domain conformation, or loss of autoinhibitory mechanisms, leading to persistent downstream signaling.

evidence.reference_title: c-KIT Small Molecule Inhibitors as a Therapeutic Strategy for Melanoma: Clinical Insights, SAR, and Future Directions.
evidence.snippet: Mutation of c-KIT has been observed in acral, mucosal, and chronically sun-damaged melanoma subtypes marking it as a key therapeutic target for melanoma.

evidence.reference_title: Somatic activation of KIT in distinct subtypes of melanoma.
evidence.snippet: mutations and/or copy number increases of KIT in 39% of mucosal, 36% of acral, and 28% of melanomas on chronically sun-damaged skin, but not in any (0%) melanomas on skin without chronic sun damage

evidence.reference_title: Therapeutic implications of KIT in melanoma.
evidence.snippet: Activating mutations in KIT have been discovered in a significant proportion of melanomas arising from acral, mucosal, and chronically sun-damaged sites and represent an important melanoma genetic subset.

evidence.reference_title: Pathological activation of KIT in metastatic tumors of acral and mucosal melanomas.
evidence.snippet: Western blot analysis showed phosphorylation of the KIT receptor in 8 (62%) of 13 cryopreserved samples, indicating the frequent pathological activation of the receptor in vivo.

nodes.description: Activated KIT recruits adapter proteins that engage RAS-MAPK signaling, driving melanocyte proliferation through ERK-mediated transcriptional programs promoting cell cycle progression.

evidence.reference_title: Imatinib targeting of KIT-mutant oncoprotein in melanoma.
evidence.snippet: Exposure to drug affected the mitogen-activated protein kinase, phosphatidylinositol 3-kinase/AKT, JAK-STAT, and antiapoptotic pathways.

nodes.description: KIT activates PI3K through recruitment and phosphorylation of adapter proteins. Subsequent AKT activation promotes cell survival through inhibition of pro-apoptotic proteins and activation of mTOR signaling.

evidence.reference_title: Imatinib targeting of KIT-mutant oncoprotein in melanoma.
evidence.snippet: Exposure to drug affected the mitogen-activated protein kinase, phosphatidylinositol 3-kinase/AKT, JAK-STAT, and antiapoptotic pathways.

nodes.description: Constitutive KIT signaling through MAPK and PI3K pathways drives abnormal melanocyte proliferation in acral and mucosal sites, leading to tumor formation.

evidence.reference_title: Imatinib targeting of KIT-mutant oncoprotein in melanoma.
evidence.snippet: Imatinib dramatically decreased proliferation and was cytotoxic to a KIT mutated and amplified cell culture.
